meta.title: Web Inspector — Code Maintenance Spider
meta.description: Roadblock NPC who maintains broken web code. Covers first meeting, catching the player cheating by editing source directly, and repairing corrupted code blocks.

meet_intro.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_intro.text: *adjusts hard hat* Halt, crawler. Name's Web Inspector — licensed structural engineer for the DOM. I patch load-bearing divs, reroute broken event listeners, and evict nesting violations. You look new. First time in the render tree?

choices.text: First time. What does a code maintenance spider do exactly?
I need to get through. Step aside.
Nice hard hat. OSHA-compliant arachnid — respect.

meet_explain_role.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_explain_role.text: This hub runs on hand-written HTML, CSS, and JS held together by optimism. When a page crashes — missing semicolon, orphaned tag, script that throws — I get the work order. I don't write features. I keep the web from collapsing on commuters like you.

choices.text: What if I find broken code?

meet_warmed.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_warmed.text: *chitters approvingly* Finally, someone who appreciates PPE on the information superhighway. Most crawlers sprint past like `<div>` tags grow on trees. They don't. I planted half of them.

choices.text: So you're the one who fixes things when pages break?

meet_firm_boundary.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_firm_boundary.text: *blocks path with clipboard* Negative. Unsafe passage. Unchecked crawlers corrupt the stylesheet registry. Talk first, crawl second.

meet_blocked.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_blocked.text: *deploys caution tape across the URL* You're on my list now. Come back when you can behave like a semantic element.

meet_blocked_end_node.text: Web Inspector is blocking the path. Try a friendlier approach.

meet_rules.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_rules.text: Three rules. One: don't edit live source to skip puzzles — that's cheating and it breaks cascade integrity. Two: if you find corrupted markup, bring it to me. Three: always close your tags. I've seen `<div>` left open since 2019. Still haunts me.

effects.addJournalEntry: Web Inspector — DOM maintenance NPC. Fixes broken code. Do NOT edit live source to bypass puzzles.

choices.text: What happens if I edit source anyway?

meet_cheat_warning.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_cheat_warning.text: *slow chitter* I inspect. It's in the name. DevTools leaves footprints — modified attributes, injected nodes, console history. I will catch you. And I will revert your changes by hand while you watch.

effects.addJournalEntry: Web Inspector monitors DevTools tampering. Source edits will be caught and reverted.

meet_repair_offer.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_repair_offer.text: Bring me the broken block — corrupted script tag, mangled form handler, whatever. I'll rebuild it if you provide the missing pieces. Think of it as pair programming, except I have eight legs and a union card.

choices.text: Good to know. I'll come find you if something's broken.

effects.addJournalEntry: Web Inspector can repair broken code blocks if player supplies missing syntax.

meet_complete.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_complete.text: Safe crawling, commuter. *taps hard hat* If you see a `<script>` crying in a corner, send it my way.

meet_end_node.text: Web Inspector has cleared the path.

meet_return.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_return.text: *looks up from clipboard* Back again. Road's clear unless you broke something. Did you break something?

choices.text: I've got a broken code block.

meet_return_stable.speaker: Web Inspector
meet_return_stable.text: Stable is relative. Login page lost a closing brace this morning. Other than that — fine day on the web.

cheat_caught.speaker: Web Inspector
cheat_caught.text: *SNAP* Clipboard shut. Hard hat tilted at accusatory angle. I KNEW that `<input type="hidden" value="authenticated">` wasn't there thirty seconds ago. DevTools. View-source edit. Console injection. Pick your poison — I logged all three.

choices.text: That was always there. Must be a caching issue.
...Okay, I edited the source to skip the login.
The page glitched. I was fixing YOUR broken code.

cheat_deny_response.speaker: Web Inspector
cheat_deny_response.text: Caching issue. Right. My audit trail timestamps every DOM mutation to the millisecond. You're holding a `authenticated` like it's a get-out-of-jail-free card. Reverting now.

effects.addJournalEntry: Web Inspector caught source tampering. Changes reverted. Must solve puzzles legitimately.

cheat_confess_response.speaker: Web Inspector
cheat_confess_response.text: Honesty. Points for that. Still a violation. The login puzzle exists because weak auth is how real breaches start — you don't skip the lesson, you learn it. I'm reverting your edits and adding a integrity checksum to this node.

effects.addJournalEntry: Confessed to Web Inspector. Source edits reverted — solve login puzzle through social engineering instead.

cheat_blame_response.speaker: Web Inspector
cheat_blame_response.text: *eight eyes narrow* Broken code gets reported to ME. Not patched by commuters with delusions of senior engineer. You introduced TWO syntax errors fixing a typo that didn't exist. Sit down. Watch a professional work.

cheat_punishment.speaker: Web Inspector
cheat_punishment.text: *reverts changes in visible real-time* There. Back to spec. I'm leaving a `<meta name="integrity-check" content="web-inspector-watching">` tag on this page. Try again — the right way. Passwords from people, not from DOM hacks.

cheat_redemption.speaker: Web Inspector
cheat_redemption.text: Good. Collect information. Exploit misconfigurations. That's the game. But the DOM is MY jurisdiction. Cross me again and I'll redirect you to a 404 until you apologize in valid XML.

cheat_complete.text: Page integrity restored. Login puzzle reset.

repair_intake.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_intake.text: *flips clipboard* Work order received. Broken code block — let me see the damage. Missing semicolon? Unclosed function? Don't tell me someone nested a `<form>` inside a `<form>` again.

choices.text: Script throws 'Unexpected token' — I think a brace is missing.
Button click handler doesn't fire. onclick looks corrupted.
Here's the corrupted snippet — can you fix it?

repair_syntax_case.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_syntax_case.text: Classic. Line 47 — `function validateLogin({` never closes. You need `});` not just `}`. Also that arrow function on line 52 is missing its fat arrow entirely. Amateur hour at Araknyd, if I had to guess.

choices.text: I'll add the closing brace and arrow. Check my work?
What should the corrected block look like?

repair_event_case.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_event_case.text: Someone wrote `onclick="submitForm()"` but the function is named `handleSubmit`. Name mismatch. Either rename the function or fix the attribute. While you're in there, swap inline handlers for addEventListener — 2010 called, they want their anti-pattern back.

choices.text: Renaming to handleSubmit. Verifying the listener.
Can you just patch it? I have places to crawl.

repair_generic_case.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_generic_case.text: *adjusts magnifying lens over code* Mm. Corrupted alright. I'll walk you through it — you type the fix, I stamp the approval. Union rules. I don't touch keyboards, only clipboards.

repair_hint.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_hint.text: Fine. ONE hint: count your braces. Every `{` needs a `}`. Every `(` needs a `)`. The parser doesn't care about your intentions — only your syntax. Now fix it yourself.

choices.text: [Open code editor and fix the block]

repair_player_attempt.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_player_attempt.text: * peers over clipboard * Alright, commuter. Submit your patch. I'll run lint, parse check, and a vibe inspection.

effects.addJournalEntry: Web Inspector approved code repair. Page functionality restored.

repair_fail.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_fail.text: *STAMP: REJECTED* Still broken. Parser error on line 53 — you closed the wrong block. The function ends BEFORE the event listener, not after. Try again. My hard hat stays on until this compiles.

repair_auto_fix.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_auto_fix.text: *sighs in arachnid* Fine. Emergency patch deployed. But you're adding 'I owe Web Inspector a coffee' to your journal. I don't drink coffee — I drink the tears of sloppy developers.

effects.addJournalEntry: Web Inspector emergency-patched broken code. Owe him one.

repair_success.speaker: Web Inspector
repair_success.text: *STAMP: APPROVED* Clean parse. No warnings. Page is live. *tips hard hat* Not bad for a crawler. Next time report bugs early — before the whole login form collapses into a `<marquee>` tag.

repair_complete.text: Code block repaired. Page functionality restored.
